title: [Connections][Search bar]: Ctrl + F hotkey doesn't work if Quick Connect toolbar is active
author: PetbkA
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
_Ctrl + F_ hotkey doesn't work if _Quick Connect_ toolbar is active. I guess there's no sense in using the hotkey for QC field so it should activate the search bar.

## Expected Behavior
<!--- If you're describing a bug, tell us what should happen -->
<!--- If you're suggesting a change/improvement, tell us how it should work -->
Pressing Ctrl + F when QC toolbar is active should activate the search bar.

## Current Beha...

iterations.description: Ctrl+F hotkey for 'Find in Session' is not working when the Quick Connect toolbar has focus due to key event handling interference.

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: I think such has been fixed in 1.77.3

comments.author: scottfarwellzt
comments.snippet:
For those of us using mRemoteNG in windows7/10 and relying on it to be stable is there some workaround to allow me to search amongst all of the connections I have spent hours adding to mRemoteNG.

I thought it auto-searched from the connect bar but evidently not.   Now that I have subfoldered my connections I could really use this.

Not a programmer so not comfortable with code.
Windows admin.
Linux admin.

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: that a bit different approach but yes I know about such, will be solved

comments.author: scottfarwellzt
comments.snippet:
I did finally notice the search bar on the bottom of the connections window
but I wish i could move it to the top where most people look for search
boxes or have the connect box be dual-function with auto-search as you type.

Searching for a string does not allow you to move to the next one or cycle
through them as it just locates the first one.

For example, all of my VMC systems have VMC appended to their name (since
they are very few) so I wanted to either search and list them all or ...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): Ctrl+F hotkey for 'Find in Session' is not working when the Quick Connect toolbar has focus due to key event handling interference.
Approach: Override ProcessCmdKey in FrmMain.cs to explicitly handle Ctrl+F and call ConnectionWindow.FindInSession().
